summary:
You are given a list of non-overlapping intervals sorted by their start value, where each interval is written as [start, end]. You are also given one new interval to add.

Insert the new interval into the list, merging it with any intervals it overlaps, and return the resulting list still sorted by start and free of overlaps.

examples.explanation: [2, 5] overlaps [1, 3], so they merge into [1, 5]; [6, 9] is untouched.
[4, 8] overlaps [3, 5], [6, 7], and [8, 10], which collapse into a single [3, 10].

constraints: Each interval is [start, end] with start <= end
The input intervals do not overlap and are sorted in ascending order by start
Two intervals are considered overlapping if they share at least one boundary point
